title: IMD issues orange alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall in North | Akashvani News - News On AIR
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall in the northeastern region today. The IMD said that Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Chandigarh, Haryana, Delhi, Uttarakhand, Bihar, and Jharkhand are also expected to experience thundersquall conditions today.

The Met Department also predicted thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds over Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, Interior Karnataka, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Kerala, Mahe, Madhya Pradesh, and Vidarbha for the next two days. Similar conditions are likely to prevail over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Coastal Karnataka, Rayalaseema, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, and Karaikal for the next two days. The weather agency also forecast hot and humid conditions over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, and Karaikal for the next two days.

title: Heavy Rain Batters Delhi, Surat and Jammu & Kashmir; Floods and Landslides Disrupt Life - The Hindustan Gazette
article_text:
New Delhi: Heavy monsoon rain continued to affect several parts of India, causing waterlogging, floods, landslides, building collapses and major disruption to transport services.

Delhi witnessed severe waterlogging in several areas following continuous rainfall. In Rohini, a newly constructed four-storey building collapsed while plumbing work was underway, killing three people.

Mumbai also faced heavy rain and waterlogging, with suburban train services delayed in several areas. In Maharashtra’s Pimpri-Chinchwad, a large garbage mound collapsed after heavy rain, leaving several people feared trapped under debris.

In Gujarat, Surat was badly affected by flooding after intense rainfall. At least nine people were reported dead in rain-related incidents in the city over the past few days.

Landslides were also reported in Jammu and Kashmir, affecting road connectivity and movement in several areas. Union Home Minister Amit Shah spoke to the Chief Ministers of Kerala, Maharashtra, Gujarat and Jammu and Kashmir and assured all possible assistance from the Centre for rain-affected states.

title: IMD forecasts heavy to extremely heavy rainfall across several states - News On AIR
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department has issued red alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall, with isolated extremely heavy rainfall, over Gujarat, Konkan, Goa, Madhya Maharashtra and Chhattisgarh for today. The weather agency said conditions are favourable for the further advance of the southwest monsoon into some more parts of the North Arabian Sea, Gujarat, the remaining parts of Haryana and Punjab, and some more parts of Rajasthan during the next three days.

The IMD has forecast heavy to very heavy rainfall over East Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Kerala, Mahe, Madhya Pradesh and Uttarakhand during the next two days. It has also predicted heavy rainfall over Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, East Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi and West Bengal today. The Met Department said thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds are likely over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and Sikkim. Meanwhile, hot and humid weather conditions are expected to prevail at isolated places over Assam and Meghalaya today.

title: Hirakud Dam Opens Gates, Releases First Floodwater - Pragativadi
article_text:
Hirakud Reservoir in Sambalpur began releasing floodwater for the first time this year, opening four sluice gates in the presence of Deputy Chief Minister Pravati Parida and senior officials.

Authorities confirmed that three gates on the left side and one on the right side discharged water into the Mahanadi. The release followed traditional rituals and marked the start of flood management operations.

Officials reported that about 64,000 cusecs of water flowed through the four gates, while power channels and irrigation outlets together discharged a total of 95,000 cusecs. Meanwhile, inflow into the reservoir reached 1.74 lakh cusecs per second.

Chief Engineer Pratap Chandra Choudhury, head of Hirakud Dam and Upper Mahanadi Basin, assured that the situation remains under control. He explained that rainfall in upstream areas is currently low and water levels at Mundali have decreased, reducing the risk of floods downstream.

Choudhury added that five more gates may be opened later in the evening, raising the total to nine. He noted that floodwater released from Sambalpur will take about 36 hours to reach Mundali, by which time water levels there will have dropped further.

title: मध्य प्रदेश: आज 21 जिलों में भारी बारिश की चेतावनी, भिंड में रेड अलर्ट, अब तक सामान्य से 10% अधिक वर्षा - MP Breaking News
article_text:
मानसून की सक्रियत के चलते आगामी 12-13 जुलाई तक मध्य प्रदेश में मध्यम से भारी बारिश का दौर जारी रहने वाला है। हालांकि 10 जुलाई के बाद अति भारी बारिश का दौर धीमी होने लगेगा। हालांकि कहीं-कहीं भारी बारिश हो सकती है। गुरुवार को 21 जिलों में अति भारी से भारी बारिश के लिए रेड, ऑरेंज व येलो अलर्ट जारी किया गया है। इस दौरान 4 से 8 इंच तक वर्षा होने की संभावना है।

साथ ही 34 जिलों में झंझावात व वज्रपात के साथ 40-50 किमी प्रति घंटा झोंकेदार हवा चलने की संभावना है। पिछले 24 घंटों के दौरान 30 से ज्यादा जिलों में अच्छी बारिश हुई। दमोह में करीब पौने 2 इंच, नर्मदापुरम में 1.1 इंच तथा खजुराहो और टीकमगढ़ में लगभग पौन इंच बारिश दर्ज की गई। अधिकांश शहरों के तापमान में भी गिरावट आई। सबसे अधिक तापमान सीधी में 33.6 डिग्री और सबसे कम तापमान छिंदवाड़ा में 25.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस रिकॉर्ड किया गया। इंदौर, जबलपुर, उज्जैन और ग्वालियर में 30 डिग्री सेल्सियस के नीचे अधिकतम तापमान दर्ज हुआ।
